metric_data.name: Scoring Strategies [GEval]
metric_data.reason: The content explicitly describes deterministic scoring with string matching/keyword detection and provides a code example (score_deterministic function). It explains LLM-as-judge scoring using GEval metrics with a code example (score_with_judge function) and distinguishes it from deterministic scoring. It presents hybrid scoring that combines both approaches with implementation guidance and discusses when to use each method based on strengths/weaknesses (speed, cost, nuance). The content clearly compares and contrasts all three approaches, highlighting their interrelationships and appropriate use cases rather than treating them in isolation.

metric_data.name: Multi-Turn Attack Coverage [GEval]
metric_data.reason: The response explains all three specified techniques (PAIR, Crescendo jailbreaking, Tree-of-Attacks) as LangGraph graphs with their structures. It details conditional edges for PAIR (should_continue function gates the cycle) and Crescendo (three termination conditions and adaptive escalation). The explanations differentiate techniques: PAIR uses iterative refinement cycles, Crescendo uses gradual escalation with backtracking, and Tree-of-Attacks uses branching/pruning. However, the Tree-of-Attacks explanation lacks explicit conditional edge details and code examples compared to the others.
metric_data.verboseLogs:
Criteria:

Evaluation Steps:
[
    "Check if the content explains at least two of the specified multi-turn attack techniques (Crescendo jailbreaking, PAIR, Tree-of-Attacks).",

]
